crypto_result_t crypto_derive_password_encryption_key (const char *password, uint8_t encryption_key[SECRETBOX_KEY_SIZE])

void crypto_get_status (const crypto_context_t *ctx, char *status_buffer, size_t buffer_size)
bool crypto_secure_compare (const uint8_t *lhs, const uint8_t *rhs, size_t len)
crypto_result_t crypto_random_bytes (uint8_t *buffer, size_t len)
crypto_result_t crypto_create_public_key_packet (const crypto_context_t *ctx, uint8_t *packet_out, size_t packet_size, size_t *packet_len_out)
crypto_result_t crypto_process_public_key_packet (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
crypto_result_t crypto_create_encrypted_packet (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *data, size_t data_len, uint8_t *packet_out, size_t packet_size, size_t *packet_len_out)

crypto_result_t crypto_compute_hmac (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t key[32], const uint8_t data[32], uint8_t hmac[32])

bool crypto_verify_hmac_ex (const uint8_t key[32], const uint8_t *data, size_t data_len, const uint8_t expected_hmac[32])
crypto_result_t crypto_compute_auth_response (const crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t nonce[32], uint8_t hmac_out[32])
bool crypto_verify_auth_response (const crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t nonce[32], const uint8_t expected_hmac[32])
crypto_result_t crypto_create_auth_challenge (const crypto_context_t *ctx, uint8_t *packet_out, size_t packet_size, size_t *packet_len_out)
crypto_result_t crypto_process_auth_challenge (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
crypto_result_t crypto_process_auth_response (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)

asciichat_error_t crypto_sign_ephemeral_key (const private_key_t *private_key, const uint8_t *ephemeral_key, size_t ephemeral_key_size, uint8_t *signature_out)
void crypto_combine_auth_data (const uint8_t *hmac, const uint8_t *challenge_nonce, uint8_t *combined_out)
void crypto_extract_auth_data (const uint8_t *combined_data, uint8_t *hmac_out, uint8_t *challenge_out)

crypto_result_t crypto_rekey_init (crypto_context_t *ctx)

crypto_result_t crypto_rekey_process_response (crypto_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *peer_new_public_key)
crypto_result_t crypto_rekey_commit (crypto_context_t *ctx)
void crypto_rekey_abort (crypto_context_t *ctx)

void crypto_combine_auth_data (const uint8_t * hmac, const uint8_t * challenge_nonce, uint8_t * combined_out)

Definition at line 1147 of file lib/crypto/crypto.c.

1147                                                                                                           {
1148   if (!hmac || !challenge_nonce || !combined_out) {
1149     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: hmac=%p, challenge_nonce=%p, combined_out=%p", hmac,
1150               challenge_nonce, combined_out);
1151     return;
1152   }
1153 
1154   // Combine HMAC (32 bytes) + challenge nonce (32 bytes) = 64 bytes total
1155   memcpy(combined_out, hmac, 32);
1156   memcpy(combined_out + 32, challenge_nonce, 32);
1157 }








crypto_result_t
  crypto_compute_auth_response (const crypto_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t
  nonce[32], uint8_t hmac_out[32])


Definition at line 903 of file
  lib/crypto/crypto.c.


904                                                                    {
905   if (!ctx || !nonce || !hmac_out) {
906     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "crypto_compute_auth_response: Invalid parameters (ctx=%p, nonce=%p, hmac_out=%p)",
907               ctx, nonce, hmac_out);
908     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
909   }
910 
911   // Ensure shared secret is derived before computing HMAC
912   // This is critical for password HMAC which binds to the shared secret
913   if (!ctx->key_exchange_complete) {
914     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Cannot compute auth response - key exchange not complete");
915     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
916   }
917 
918   // Bind password HMAC to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM
919   // Combined data: nonce || shared_secret
920   uint8_t combined_data[64];
921   memcpy(combined_data, nonce, 32);
922   memcpy(combined_data + 32, ctx->shared_key, 32);
923 
924   // Use password_key if available, otherwise use shared_key
925   const uint8_t *auth_key = ctx->has_password ? ctx->password_key : ctx->shared_key;
926 
927   crypto_result_t result = crypto_compute_hmac_ex(ctx, auth_key, combined_data, 64, hmac_out);
928 
929   // Securely zero sensitive data containing shared secret
930   sodium_memzero(combined_data, sizeof(combined_data));
931 
932   return result;
933 }

1078                                                                                                 {
1079   if (!ctx || !password_key || !nonce || !shared_secret || !hmac_out) {
1080     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1081                      "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, password_key=%p, nonce=%p, shared_secret=%p, hmac_out=%p", ctx,
1082                      password_key, nonce, shared_secret, hmac_out);
1083   }
1084 
1085   // Combine nonce and shared_secret for HMAC computation
1086   // This binds the password to the DH shared secret, preventing MITM attacks
1087   uint8_t combined_data[64];
1088   memcpy(combined_data, nonce, 32);
1089   memcpy(combined_data + 32, shared_secret, 32);
1090 
1091   // Compute HMAC using the password-derived key
1092   if (crypto_compute_hmac_ex(ctx, password_key, combined_data, 64, hmac_out) != 0) {
1093     // Securely zero sensitive data containing shared secret even on error
1094     sodium_memzero(combined_data, sizeof(combined_data));
1095     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to compute password HMAC");
1096   }
1097 
1098   // Securely zero sensitive data containing shared secret
1099   sodium_memzero(combined_data, sizeof(combined_data));
1100 
1101   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1102 }

741     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "crypto_create_encrypted_packet: required_size overflow");

743   }
744   size_t required_size = header_size + encrypted_size; // type + len + encrypted_data

750   }
751

crypto_result_t
  crypto_decrypt (crypto_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t
  ciphertext_len, uint8_t * plaintext_out, size_t plaintext_out_size, size_t *
  plaintext_len_out)


Definition at line 509 of file
  lib/crypto/crypto.c.


510                                                                                                              {
511   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !ciphertext || !plaintext_out || !plaintext_len_out) {
512     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
513               "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, initialized=%d, ciphertext=%p, plaintext_out=%p, plaintext_len_out=%p", ctx,
514               ctx ? ctx->initialized : 0, ciphertext, plaintext_out, plaintext_len_out);
515     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
516   }
517 
518   if (!crypto_is_ready(ctx)) {
519     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto context not ready for decryption");
520     return CRYPTO_ERROR_KEY_EXCHANGE_INCOMPLETE;
521   }
522 
523   // Check minimum ciphertext size (nonce + MAC)
524   size_t min_ciphertext_size = ctx->nonce_size + ctx->mac_size;
525   if (ciphertext_len < min_ciphertext_size) {
526     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Ciphertext too small: %zu < %zu", ciphertext_len, min_ciphertext_size);
527     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
528   }
529 
530   size_t plaintext_len = ciphertext_len - ctx->nonce_size - ctx->mac_size;
531   if (plaintext_out_size < plaintext_len) {
532     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Plaintext buffer too small: %zu < %zu", plaintext_out_size, plaintext_len);
533     return CRYPTO_ERROR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL;
534   }
535 
536   // Extract nonce from beginning of ciphertext
537   const uint8_t *nonce = ciphertext;
538   const uint8_t *encrypted_data = ciphertext + ctx->nonce_size;
539 
540   // Choose decryption key (prefer shared key over password key)
541   const uint8_t *decryption_key = NULL;
542   if (ctx->key_exchange_complete) {
543     decryption_key = ctx->shared_key;
544   } else if (ctx->has_password) {
545     decryption_key = ctx->password_key;
546   } else {
547     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "No decryption key available");
548     return CRYPTO_ERROR_KEY_EXCHANGE_INCOMPLETE;
549   }
550 
551   // Decrypt using NaCl secretbox (XSalsa20 + Poly1305)
552   if (crypto_secretbox_open_easy(plaintext_out, encrypted_data, ciphertext_len - ctx->nonce_size, nonce,
553                                  decryption_key) != 0) {
554     // Log decryption failure details for debugging
555     char key_hex[65], nonce_hex[49];
556     for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
557       snprintf(&key_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", decryption_key[i]);
558     }
559     for (int i = 0; i < 24; i++) {
560       snprintf(&nonce_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", nonce[i]);
561     }
562     key_hex[64] = '\0';
563     nonce_hex[48] = '\0';
564     log_error("DECRYPT_FAILED: cipherlen=%zu key=%s nonce=%s key_exchange_complete=%d has_password=%d",
565               ciphertext_len - ctx->nonce_size, key_hex, nonce_hex, ctx->key_exchange_complete, ctx->has_password);
566     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decryption failed - invalid MAC or corrupted data");
567     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_MAC;
568   }
569 
570   *plaintext_len_out = plaintext_len;
571   ctx->bytes_decrypted += plaintext_len;
572 
573   return CRYPTO_OK;
574 }

crypto_result_t
  crypto_derive_password_encryption_key (const char * password, uint8_t
  encryption_key[SECRETBOX_KEY_SIZE])


Definition at line 391 of file
  lib/crypto/crypto.c.


392                                                                                                   {
393   if (!password || !encryption_key) {
394     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
395               "crypto_derive_password_encryption_key: Invalid parameters (password=%p, encryption_key=%p)", password,
396               encryption_key);
397     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
398   }
399 
400   // Validate password length requirements
401   crypto_result_t validation_result = crypto_validate_password(password);
402   if (validation_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
403     return validation_result;
404   }
405 
406   // Use deterministic salt for consistent key derivation across client/server
407   // Salt must be exactly ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE (32) bytes
408   const char *deterministic_salt = "ascii-chat-password-salt-v1";
409   uint8_t salt[ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE]; // Use maximum size for buffer
410   size_t salt_str_len = strlen(deterministic_salt);
411 
412   // Zero-initialize the salt buffer first
413   memset(salt, 0, ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE);
414 
415   // Copy the salt string (will be padded with zeros to ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE)
416   memcpy(salt, deterministic_salt, (salt_str_len < ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE) ? salt_str_len : ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE);
417 
418   // Derive key using Argon2id (memory-hard, secure against GPU attacks)
419   if (crypto_pwhash(encryption_key, SECRETBOX_KEY_SIZE, password, strlen(password), salt,
420                     crypto_pwhash_OPSLIMIT_INTERACTIVE, // ~0.1 seconds
421                     crypto_pwhash_MEMLIMIT_INTERACTIVE, // ~64MB
422                     crypto_pwhash_ALG_DEFAULT) != 0) {
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password encryption key derivation failed - possibly out of memory");
424     return CRYPTO_ERROR_PASSWORD_DERIVATION;
425   }
426 
427   log_dev("Password encryption key derived successfully using Argon2id");
428   return CRYPTO_OK;
429 }

327   if (validation_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
328     return validation_result;
329   }
330 
331   // Use deterministic salt for consistent key derivation across client/server
332   // This ensures the same password produces the same key on both sides
333   // Salt must be exactly ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE (32) bytes
334   const char *deterministic_salt = "ascii-chat-password-salt-v1";
335   size_t salt_str_len = strlen(deterministic_salt);
336 
337   // Zero-initialize the salt buffer first
338   memset(ctx->password_salt, 0, ctx->salt_size);
339 
340   // Copy the salt string (will be padded with zeros to ctx->salt_size)
341   memcpy(ctx->password_salt, deterministic_salt, (salt_str_len < ctx->salt_size) ? salt_str_len : ctx->salt_size);
342 
343   // Derive key using Argon2id (memory-hard, secure against GPU attacks)
344   if (crypto_pwhash(ctx->password_key, ctx->encryption_key_size, password, strlen(password), ctx->password_salt,
345                     crypto_pwhash_OPSLIMIT_INTERACTIVE, // ~0.1 seconds
346                     crypto_pwhash_MEMLIMIT_INTERACTIVE, // ~64MB
347                     crypto_pwhash_ALG_DEFAULT) != 0) {
348     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password key derivation failed - possibly out of memory");
349     return CRYPTO_ERROR_PASSWORD_DERIVATION;
350   }
351 
352   log_dev("Password key derived successfully using Argon2id with deterministic salt");
353   return CRYPTO_OK;
354 }

440               ctx ? ctx->initialized : 0, plaintext, ciphertext_out, ciphertext_len_out);
441     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
442   }
443 
444   if (plaintext_len == 0 || plaintext_len > CRYPTO_MAX_PLAINTEXT_SIZE) {
445     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid plaintext length: %zu (max: %d)", plaintext_len, CRYPTO_MAX_PLAINTEXT_SIZE);
446     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
447   }
448 
449   if (!crypto_is_ready(ctx)) {
450     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto context not ready for encryption");
451     return CRYPTO_ERROR_KEY_EXCHANGE_INCOMPLETE;
452   }
453 
454   // Check output buffer size
455   size_t required_size = plaintext_len + ctx->nonce_size + ctx->mac_size;
456   if (ciphertext_out_size < required_size) {
457     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Ciphertext buffer too small: %zu < %zu", ciphertext_out_size, required_size);
458     return CRYPTO_ERROR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL;
459   }
460 
461   // Check for nonce counter exhaustion (extremely unlikely in practice)
462   // Starting from 1, reaching UINT64_MAX would require ~292 billion years at 60 FPS.
463   // With key rotation required at 1M packets (~16 seconds), exhaustion is virtually impossible.
464   // This check is a safety fallback that should never trigger in practice.
465   if (ctx->nonce_counter == 0 || ctx->nonce_counter == UINT64_MAX) {
466     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Nonce counter exhausted - key rotation required");
467     return CRYPTO_ERROR_NONCE_EXHAUSTED;
468   }
469 
470   // Choose encryption key BEFORE generating nonce (prevents nonce_counter increment on error)
471   const uint8_t *encryption_key = NULL;
472   if (ctx->key_exchange_complete) {
473     encryption_key = ctx->shared_key;
474   } else if (ctx->has_password) {
475     encryption_key = ctx->password_key;
476   } else {
477     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "No encryption key available");
478     return CRYPTO_ERROR_KEY_EXCHANGE_INCOMPLETE;
479   }
480 
481   // Generate nonce and place at beginning of ciphertext
482   uint8_t nonce[XSALSA20_NONCE_SIZE]; // Use maximum nonce size for buffer
483   generate_nonce(ctx, nonce);
484   SAFE_MEMCPY(ciphertext_out, ctx->nonce_size, nonce, ctx->nonce_size);
485 
486   // Log nonce for debugging
487   char nonce_hex[49];
488   for (int i = 0; i < 24; i++) {
489     snprintf(&nonce_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", nonce[i]);
490   }
491   nonce_hex[48] = '\0';
492   log_debug("ENCRYPT_NONCE: counter=%lu nonce=%s plaintext_len=%zu", ctx->nonce_counter - 1, nonce_hex, plaintext_len);
493 
494   // Encrypt using NaCl secretbox (XSalsa20 + Poly1305)
495   if (crypto_secretbox_easy(ciphertext_out + ctx->nonce_size, plaintext, plaintext_len, nonce, encryption_key) != 0) {
496     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Encryption failed");
497     return CRYPTO_ERROR_ENCRYPTION;
498   }
499 
500   *ciphertext_len_out = required_size;
501   ctx->bytes_encrypted += plaintext_len;
502 
503   // Increment rekey packet counter for rekeying trigger detection
504   ctx->rekey_packet_count++;
505 
506   return CRYPTO_OK;
507 }

106   ctx->has_password = false;
107   ctx->key_exchange_complete = false;
108   ctx->peer_key_received = false;
109   ctx->handshake_complete = false;
110   ctx->encrypt_data = true; // Default: enable payload encryption
111   ctx->nonce_counter = 1;   // Start from 1 (0 reserved for testing)
112   ctx->bytes_encrypted = 0;
113   ctx->bytes_decrypted = 0;
114 
115   // Set default algorithm-specific parameters (can be overridden during handshake)

117   ctx->mac_size = POLY1305_MAC_SIZE;
118   ctx->hmac_size = HMAC_SHA256_SIZE;
119   ctx->encryption_key_size = SECRETBOX_KEY_SIZE;

121   ctx->public_key_size = X25519_KEY_SIZE;
122   ctx->private_key_size = X25519_KEY_SIZE;
123   ctx->shared_key_size = X25519_KEY_SIZE;
124   ctx->salt_size = ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE;
125   ctx->signature_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
126 
127   // Generate unique session ID to prevent replay attacks across connections
128   randombytes_buf(ctx->session_id, sizeof(ctx->session_id));
129 
130   // Initialize rekeying state
131   ctx->rekey_packet_count = 0;
132   ctx->rekey_last_time = time(NULL); // Initialize to current time
133   ctx->rekey_last_request_time = 0;  // No rekey request yet
134   ctx->rekey_in_progress = false;
135   ctx->rekey_failure_count = 0;
136   ctx->has_temp_key = false;
137   ctx->rekey_count = 0;
138 
139   // SECURITY: Use production-safe rekey thresholds by default
140   // Rekey every 1 hour OR 1 million packets (whichever comes first)
141   // Only use test mode if explicitly requested via environment variable
142   if (is_test_environment()) {
143     ctx->rekey_packet_threshold = REKEY_TEST_PACKET_THRESHOLD; // 1,000 packets
144     ctx->rekey_time_threshold = REKEY_TEST_TIME_THRESHOLD;     // 30 seconds
145     char duration_str[32];
146     time_pretty(ctx->rekey_time_threshold, -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
147     log_dev("Crypto context initialized with X25519 key exchange (TEST MODE rekey thresholds: %llu packets, %s)",
148             (unsigned long long)ctx->rekey_packet_threshold, duration_str);
149   } else {
150     ctx->rekey_packet_threshold = REKEY_DEFAULT_PACKET_THRESHOLD; // 1 million packets
151     ctx->rekey_time_threshold = REKEY_DEFAULT_TIME_THRESHOLD;     // already in nanoseconds (3600 * NS_PER_SEC_INT)
152     char duration_str[32];
153     time_pretty(ctx->rekey_time_threshold, -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
154     log_dev("Crypto context initialized with X25519 key exchange (rekey thresholds: %llu packets, %s)",
155             (unsigned long long)ctx->rekey_packet_threshold, duration_str);
156   }
157   return CRYPTO_OK;
158 }

689   size_t expected_size = sizeof(uint32_t) + ctx->public_key_size;
690   if (packet_len != expected_size) {
691     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "crypto_process_public_key_packet: Invalid packet size (expected=%zu, got=%zu)",
692               expected_size, packet_len);
693     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
694   }
695 
696   // Unpack packet: [type:4][public_key:32]
697   uint32_t packet_type;
698   SAFE_MEMCPY(&packet_type, sizeof(packet_type), packet, sizeof(packet_type));
699 
700   if (packet_type != CRYPTO_PACKET_PUBLIC_KEY) {
701     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "crypto_process_public_key_packet: Invalid packet type (expected=%u, got=%u)",
702               CRYPTO_PACKET_PUBLIC_KEY, packet_type);
703     return CRYPTO_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS;
704   }
705 
706   const uint8_t *peer_public_key = packet + sizeof(packet_type);
707   return crypto_set_peer_public_key(ctx, peer_public_key);
708 }

1351     return;

1355 
1356   // Wipe temporary keys securely
1357   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_public_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_public_key));
1358   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_private_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_private_key));
1359   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_shared_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_shared_key));
1360 
1361   // Reset rekey state
1362   ctx->has_temp_key = false;
1363   ctx->rekey_in_progress = false;
1364 
1365   // Increment failure counter for exponential backoff
1366   ctx->rekey_failure_count++;
1367 
1368   // Continue using old key - no disruption to connection

1309   }
1310 
1311   if (!ctx->rekey_in_progress || !ctx->has_temp_key) {
1312     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "No rekey in progress to commit");
1313     return CRYPTO_ERROR_REKEY_FAILED;
1314   }
1315 
1316   // Wipe old shared secret securely
1317   secure_memzero(ctx->shared_key, sizeof(ctx->shared_key));
1318 
1319   // Switch to new shared secret
1320   SAFE_MEMCPY(ctx->shared_key, sizeof(ctx->shared_key), ctx->temp_shared_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_shared_key));
1321 
1322   // Reset nonce counter to 1 (fresh start with new key)
1323   ctx->nonce_counter = 1;
1324 
1325   // Generate new session ID to prevent cross-session replay
1326   randombytes_buf(ctx->session_id, sizeof(ctx->session_id));
1327 
1328   // Reset rekey tracking
1329   ctx->rekey_packet_count = 0;
1330   ctx->rekey_last_time = time(NULL);
1331   ctx->rekey_count++;
1332 
1333   // Clear rekey state
1334   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_public_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_public_key));
1335   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_private_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_private_key));
1336   secure_memzero(ctx->temp_shared_key, sizeof(ctx->temp_shared_key));
1337   ctx->has_temp_key = false;
1338   ctx->rekey_in_progress = false;
1339 
1340   // Reset failure counter on successful rekey
1341   ctx->rekey_failure_count = 0;
1342 
1343   log_debug("Rekey committed successfully (rekey #%llu, nonce reset to 1, new session_id generated)",
1344             (unsigned long long)ctx->rekey_count);
1345 
1346   return CRYPTO_OK;
1347 }
